caption: The video appears to be an educational tutorial on cryogenic engineering, specifically focusing on Tutorial 4. The presentation slide shown is titled "Cryogenic Engineering" and discusses the work per unit mass of gas liquefied and the Figure of Merit (FOM). Initially, the slide displays calculations for the work per unit mass of gas liquefied, with values such as \( \frac{W_c}{\dot{m}} = 486.2 \) and \( y = 0.0215 \), leading to \( \frac{W_c}{\dot{m}_f} = 22613.95 \, J/g \). As the video progresses, additional information is revealed, showing another value \( \frac{W_l}{\dot{m}_f} = 761 \). The FOM is then calculated using the formula \( FOM = \frac{\frac{W_l}{\dot{m}_f}}{\frac{W_c}{\dot{m}_f}} \), resulting in a value of 0.0336. The tutorial is presented by Prof. M D Atrey from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Bombay, as indicated at the bottom of the slide. The content is part of an NPTEL lecture series, aimed at teaching principles and calculations relevant to cryogenic engineering processes.
